    22|export default function HomeScreen() {

    27|  const heroScale = useRef(new Animated.Value(1.1)).current;
    28|
    29|  useEffect(() => {

    58|        contentContainerStyle={{ paddingBottom: 40 }}

    64|              style={styles.heroImage}
    65|              contentFit="cover"
    66|            />
    67|          </Animated.View>
    68|          <View style={styles.heroOverlay} />
    69|          <Animated.View
    70|            style={[

    73|                paddingTop: insets.top + 20,
    74|                opacity: fadeAnim,
    75|                transform: [{ translateY: slideAnim }],
    76|              },
    77|            ]}
    78|          >
    79|            <View style={styles.heroBadge}>
    80|              <Sparkles size={14} color={Colors.accent} />
    81|              <Text style={styles.heroBadgeText}>Premium Curb Design</Text>
    82|            </View>
    83|            <Text style={styles.heroTitle}>
    84|              Elevate Your{'\n'}Curb Appeal
    85|            </Text>
    86|            <Text style={styles.heroSubtitle}>
    87|              Custom concrete curbing that transforms{'\n'}ordinary landscapes into stunning exteriors
    88|            </Text>

    95|              <ArrowRight size={18} color={Colors.white} />
    96|            </Pressable>
    97|          </Animated.View>
    98|        </View>
    99|
   100|        <View style={styles.statsBar}>
   101|          {[
   102|            { value: '2,500+', label: 'Projects' },
   103|            { value: '15+', label: 'Years' },
   104|            { value: '4.9', label: 'Rating' },
   105|          ].map((stat, index) => (

   109|            </View>
   110|          ))}
   111|        </View>
   112|
   113|        <View style={styles.section}>

   121|            horizontal

   123|            contentContainerStyle={styles.designScroll}
   124|          >

   159|          </View>
   160|        </View>
   161|

   165|            <Pressable onPress={() => handlePress('/testimonials')}>

   169|          {testimonials.slice(0, 2).map((t) => (

    43|      </View>

    45|      <View style={styles.storySection}>
    46|        <Text style={styles.storyText}>
    47|          CurbCraft Design was born from a simple belief: that every home deserves beautiful, lasting landscape borders. Founded in 2010 by Michael Carter, a landscape architect with a passion for precision, we started as a small team in Austin, Texas.
    48|        </Text>
    49|        <Text style={styles.storyText}>
    50|          Over the past 15 years, we've grown into one of the most trusted curb design companies in the Southwest, completing over 2,500 projects across five states. Our success is built on a foundation of quality materials, skilled craftsmanship, and genuine care for every client's vision.
    51|        </Text>
    52|        <Text style={styles.storyText}>
    53|          Today, our team of experienced installers brings the same dedication and artistry to every project — whether it's a simple mower edge or an elaborate flagstone border for a luxury property.
    54|        </Text>
    55|      </View>
    56|
    57|      <View style={styles.valuesSection}>
    58|        <Text style={styles.sectionTitle}>Our Values</Text>
    59|        <View style={styles.valuesGrid}>
    60|          {values.map((v, i) => (
    61|            <View key={i} style={styles.valueCard}>
    62|              <View style={styles.valueIconWrap}>
    63|                <v.icon size={22} color={Colors.accent} />
    64|              </View>
    65|              <Text style={styles.valueTitle}>{v.title}</Text>
    66|              <Text style={styles.valueDesc}>{v.desc}</Text>
    67|            </View>
    68|          ))}
    69|        </View>
    70|      </View>
    71|
    72|      <View style={styles.teamSection}>
    73|        <Text style={styles.sectionTitle}>Meet the Team</Text>
    74|        {teamMembers.map((member) => (
    75|          <View key={member.id} style={styles.teamCard}>

    81|            <View style={styles.teamInfo}>

    84|              <Text style={styles.teamBio}>{member.bio}</Text>
    85|            </View>
    86|          </View>
    87|        ))}
    88|      </View>
    89|
    90|      <View style={styles.ctaSection}>

    95|        <Pressable

    97|          onPress={() => router.push('/contact')}
    98|        >
    99|          <Text style={styles.ctaBtnText}>Get in Touch</Text>
   100|        </Pressable>
   101|      </View>
   102|    </ScrollView>

responseMessageContents.text:
**Planning curb images**

I need to consider using the attached image URL for curb images, but I might also look for additional curb images through web searches. Since the user is asking for a design company focused on curb address markers, I should clarify if I need to replace all images with similar curb address markers. It might be helpful to search for painted address curbs while still considering using the provided attachment for design images.**Reviewing image usage**

I need to gather all image usages and possibly find more files, focusing on design images and hero sections. The user has specifically requested to replace only example photos, so I should look for curb address marker photos that feature grass or concrete. I might have to search for usable URLs directly, but that approach might involve some complexity. 

Using the same attached photo across design cards and hero sections could streamline things. Additionally, I can update design type images while keeping certain avatars consistent.

input.query: decorative painted curb address number concrete curb house number image 2026

output.context:
Decorative painted curb address numbers, such as **2026**, are created on concrete curbs using stencils for a clean, professional look, often with a contrasting background color.[1][2][3] Custom 4-inch tall stencils are available in modern fonts (e.g., mid-century Neutra Face) and can be ordered with your specific numbers like 2026.[1][3]

### DIY Painting Process
Follow these steps from professional tutorials and product guides:
- **Clean the curb**: Remove dirt, grass, or loose paint using a brush.[2][3]
- **Mask and paint background**: Tape off the area (e.g., with cardboard or paper to block overspray), then apply a base color like white or satin white in light coats using spray paint (e.g., Krylon). Let it dry fully.[1][2][3]
- **Apply stencil**: Position the custom stencil for "2026" (free downloadable or paid options available), add extra tape for borders, and spray or brush the number color (e.g., black, blue like Rustoleum Satin Lagoon) in very light coats to avoid bleeding or runs. Optional: Add reflective paint, glitter, or glass beads while wet for visibility.[2][3]
- **Dry and reveal**: Allow thorough drying, then peel off tape and stencil slowly.[2][3]

**Visual description from sources**: Images and video show crisp white rectangular backgrounds (about 1-2 feet wide) with bold black or colored 4-5 inch numbers centered on gray concrete curbs, often with clean edges and a modern, Palm Springs-style aesthetic. Neighbors compliment the sleek results.[3] A 5-minute YouTube demo demonstrates taping, spraying white base, stenciling black numbers, and final reveal on a curbside.[2]

### Alternatives to Painting
- **Decals/stickers**: Pre-made custom curb decals (e.g., VWAQ designs) stick directly onto concrete without painting or stencils—just enter "2026" and apply.[4]

**Organizing image URLs**

I have some image URLs to work with, including attachments and a few found in the search results. It seems like I can use these directly. I'll consider centralizing constants in the mocks/data and create image constants at the top. I should also check the start of my file; maybe I’m missing an import based on the first few lines. Let’s read those top lines to figure this out.

**Editing image constants**

I think I need to edit the code a bit. It might be better to create constants for images in the data and import them into home and about. Using something like HERO_IMAGE and ABOUT_IMAGE could make it simpler. Although I could just hardcode them, I think it’s worth replacing those six URLs in the data file with a mix of provided ones and similar alternatives. So, let's go ahead and update those sources for home and about!
